content.problemStatement:
For any zero-indexed sequence SSS of length NNN, define a function G(S)G(S)G(S) as follows:

G(S)=‚àèi=0N‚àí1(Si+i)G(S) = \prod_{i=0}^{N-1} (S_i + i)G(S)=i=0‚àèN‚àí1‚Äã(Si‚Äã+i)If N=0N = 0N=0, then G(S)=1G(S) = 1G(S)=1, i.e. the empty sequence has a value of 111.

For an array BBB and a parameter KKK, we also define another function F(B,K)F(B, K)F(B,K) as follows:

F(B,K)=‚àëG(S)F(B, K) = \sum G(S)F(B,K)=‚àëG(S)where the summation is taken over all subsequences SSS of BBB such that ‚à£S‚à£=K|S| = K‚à£S‚à£=K.

You have an array BBB, which is initially empty.

For each iii from 111 to NNN, solve the following problem:


You are given two integers XiX_iXi‚Äã and KiK_iKi‚Äã.

First, insert XiX_iXi‚Äã to the **front** of array BBB; so that we now have B=[Xi,Xi‚àí1,‚Ä¶,X1]B = [X_i, X_{i-1}, \ldots, X_1]B=[Xi‚Äã,Xi‚àí1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,X1‚Äã].

Then, compute
‚àëj=0Ki(Ki+1‚àíj)2‚ãÖF(B,j)\sum_{j = 0}^{K_i} (K_i + 1 - j)^2 \cdot F(B, j)j=0‚àëKi‚Äã‚Äã(Ki‚Äã+1‚àíj)2‚ãÖF(B,j)Since the answers can be very large, output them modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The iii-th of the next NNN lines contains two integers XiX_iXi‚Äã and KiK_iKi‚Äã, denoting the iii-th element to be inserted into AAA and the parameter for the iii-th query, respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines - the iii-th line must contain the answer corresponding to KiK_iKi‚Äã after insertion of XiX_iXi‚Äã to the **front** of BBB, modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases dosen't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

After the first insertion, B=[1]B = [1]B=[1].
Its subsequences are [],[1][], [1][],[1]; so we have F(B,0)=1,F(B,1)=1F(B, 0) = 1, F(B, 1) = 1F(B,0)=1,F(B,1)=1.
The answer is 4‚ãÖF(B,0)+1‚ãÖF(B,1)=54 \cdot F(B, 0) + 1 \cdot F(B, 1) = 54‚ãÖF(B,0)+1‚ãÖF(B,1)=5.
After the second insertion, B=[2,1]B = [2, 1]B=[2,1].
We have F(B,0)=1,F(B,1)=3,F(B,2)=4F(B, 0) = 1, F(B, 1) = 3, F(B, 2) = 4F(B,0)=1,F(B,1)=3,F(B,2)=4.
The answer is 9‚ãÖF(B,0)+4‚ãÖF(B,1)+1‚ãÖF(B,0)=259 \cdot F(B, 0) + 4 \cdot F(B, 1) + 1 \cdot F(B, 0) = 259‚ãÖF(B,0)+4‚ãÖF(B,1)+1‚ãÖF(B,0)=25.
